sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_chars (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, char_code INTEGER NOT NULL, char_value VARCHAR(1));
INSERT INTO test_chars (char_code, char_value) VALUES (65, chr(65)), (66, chr(66)), (67, chr(67)), (68, chr(68));
SELECT char_code, char_value FROM test_chars WHERE char_code IN (SELECT MAX(char_code) FROM test_chars);
SELECT chr(ASCII(SUBSTRING(char_value, 1, 1))) AS first_char, COUNT(*) FROM test_chars GROUP BY first_char HAVING first_char IN (chr(65), chr(66));
CREATE TABLE operations (op_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, left_operand INTEGER, right_operand INTEGER, result VARCHAR);

INSERT INTO operations (left_operand, right_operand, result) SELECT a.char_code, b.char_code, chr(a.char_code + b.char_code) FROM test_chars a JOIN test_chars b ON a.char_code IN (SELECT MAX(char_code) FROM test_chars) AND b.char_code < 70;
SELECT o.op_id, o.result FROM operations o WHERE o.result IN (SELECT DISTINCT chr(char_code) FROM test_chars WHERE char_value IS NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE mixed_types (num_col INTEGER, str_col VARCHAR);
SELECT chr(65) AS letter, COUNT(*) FROM mixed_types WHERE num_col IN (SELECT num_col FROM mixed_types WHERE str_col IS NOT NULL) GROUP BY letter HAVING COUNT(*) > 0;
